channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-07-02
item.description:
Our community has seen exciting progress across multiple projects recently. In the scienceverse/metacheck project, a significant update was made to prevent double Crossref API calls and added error logging, which will improve the handling of API interactions and aid in debugging. Meanwhile, the Davidvandijcke/coarse project has made notable strides with localization updates, including a localized confirmation email and review page, as well as deployment fixes. Interestingly, the removal of the sign-up feature in the JamieCummins/regcheck project suggests a shift in focus or approach. These updates demonstrate the interconnected nature of our projects, with improvements in one area potentially influencing others, such as the use of API calls and localization. Overall, it's great to see our community driving progress and collaboration across these open-source research tools.

35 commits across 4 active repositories this week.

Most active:

regcheck20 commits
escicheck6 commits
docpluck5 commits
metacheck4 commits

item.title: metacheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The `ref_update` branch was worked on, where a change was made to prevent double Crossref API calls, as stated in the commit message "Lakens: prevent double crossref api, log errors". This commit also added error logging for the Crossref API, as indicated by "log errors". No other branches were modified in this set of commits. The specific feature changed was the handling of Crossref API calls to prevent duplicates. The commit message explicitly mentions "Lakens", suggesting this change is related to the Lakens functionality. The logging of errors is now included to potentially aid in debugging or monitoring API interactions.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 41a54be [ref_update] prevent double crossref api, log errors — Lakens
  • bb7a5ee [ref_update] updated ref_accuracy, validated. Minor fixes to reg_check and ref_consistency — Lakens
  • 6937e9c [dev] Merge exact_p_zero_warning: flag p-values reported as exactly zero — Lakens
  • 3c854b3 [dev] Rewrite OSF prereg extraction to schema-driven dispatch — Lakens
  • 32659a9 [main] Point home page text to the Metacheck Manual — Lakens
  • d6a7f12 [main] Replace Get Started/Reference/Articles navbar with Metacheck Manual link — Lakens
  • b8ac461 [dev] Fix coi_check/funding_check cold-start failure — Lakens
  • de41849 [dev] Add R/import-papers.R so dev installs cleanly — Lakens
  • ed8c8d3 [dev] Merge pull request #317 from scienceverse/papers_databases — Lisa DeBruine
  • e077134 [dev] Merge pull request #316 from scienceverse/ethics_check — Lisa DeBruine
  • a40d7db [dev] Merge pull request #309 from scienceverse/prereg_regcheck — Lisa DeBruine
  • 1fb2bab [dev] Merge pull request #318 from scienceverse/fix/code-check-paperlist — Lisa DeBruine
  • 8cbef44 [dev] Fix code_check crash and missing paper_id on paper lists — Lakens
  • b7afa6a [dev] add functions to load paper databases from scienceverse/papers — Lakens
  • 25c15a6 [main] update metacheck page to fix #314 — Lakens
  • 73736b0 [dev] Fix create_report_app() to report_app() in Get Started vignette — Daniel Lakens
  • 00d7953 [dev] add ethics_check + bug fix — Lakens
  • 2b8a367 [new_modules] Revert "fix app instruction #314" — Lakens
  • 1d3719b [new_modules] fix app instruction #314 — Lakens
  • dccb9f4 [main] update module names in vignettes — Lakens

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

The `staging` branch was worked on, where the "sign-up" feature was removed, as per the commit message "remove sign-up". No other branches were modified in this set of commits. The removal of the sign-up feature is the only change made in this update. There are no additions or changes to any modules or other features in this set of commits. The commit message explicitly mentions the removal of sign-up, with no other changes or additions noted. Only one commit exists in this set, which is focused solely on removing the sign-up functionality from the `staging` branch.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 1f6986b [staging] remove sign-up — Jamie Cummins
  • 717d756 [staging] user survey improvment — Jamie Cummins
  • 8c5b3bf [staging] additional aesthetic imprvoements — Jamie Cummins
  • adef1de [staging] minor aesthetic improvements — Jamie Cummins
  • a3141f9 [staging] fix quote tracing — Jamie Cummins
  • a8a5cbe [staging] improve report retention — Jamie Cummins
  • d6c7e96 [staging] add multi-file upload — Jamie Cummins
  • 72f7d60 [staging] add advanced error handling and regeneration — Jamie Cummins
  • f0792b4 [staging] add html export for report; various QOL improvements — Jamie Cummins
  • ae1de84 [staging] hardening multi-study extractions — Jamie Cummins
  • 2516dd5 [staging] Update .gitignore — Jamie Cummins
  • d1ee701 [staging] improve report text normalisation — Jamie Cummins
  • fc0bb51 [staging] fix quote pop-ups — Jamie Cummins
  • a8b610a [staging] update quote chunking and UI output — Jamie Cummins
  • c920500 [staging] embeddings experimentation — Jamie Cummins
  • d5e2697 [staging] update report render — Jamie Cummins
  • e3f869b [staging] improvements based on Matilda suggestions — Jamie Cummins
  • c682ed0 [staging] add finishing touches on multistudy cli — Jamie Cummins
  • fdfb805 [staging] improve local reporting — Jamie Cummins
  • 31fa103 [staging] improve multistudy ingestion — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The main branch was the focus of work, with two commits made to it. The first commit, as stated by Gilad Feldman, was to "sync from escicheckapp@edb3c7b (effectcheck v0.6.12)" which brought the repository in line with version 0.6.12 of effectcheck. Prior to this, another commit was made to the main branch to "sync from escicheckapp@928a5bc (effectcheck v0.6.11)", synchronizing with version 0.6.11 of effectcheck. No other branches were worked on during this time. These commits specifically updated the repository to match the changes in effectcheck versions 0.6.11 and 0.6.12.

1 commit(s) today:

  • e09dbc9 [main] sync from escicheckapp@edb3c7b (effectcheck v0.6.12) (#25) — Gilad Feldman
  • bedc262 [main] sync from escicheckapp@928a5bc (effectcheck v0.6.11) (#24) — Gilad Feldman
  • cc5cfd0 [main] sync from escicheckapp@1d4c2dd (effectcheck v0.6.9) (#23) — Gilad Feldman
  • 75ba643 [main] sync from escicheckapp@26f8430 (effectcheck v0.6.8) (#22) — Gilad Feldman
  • a154bdb [main] sync from escicheckapp@57a791d (effectcheck v0.6.7) (#21) — Gilad Feldman
  • 244b2d8 [main] sync from escicheckapp@f37b5a1 (effectcheck v0.6.6) (#20) — Gilad Feldman
  • a588210 [main] sync from escicheckapp@bd6f007 (effectcheck v0.6.5) (#19) — Gilad Feldman
  • 1d48c4e [main] sync from escicheckapp@dec37b1 (effectcheck v0.6.4) (#18) — Gilad Feldman
  • 4be3331 [main] sync from escicheckapp@f52d7ce (effectcheck v0.6.3) (#17) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →
